methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
INERATEC is a Series B climate & energy technology company that has raised $129M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public (Lonza, LNZA) and 8 private companies—spanning the same sector and stage. Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc. form the core reference set.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using the $129M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $245M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparable companies produces a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B (the wide range reflects heterogeneity in capital efficiency and technology stage within climate tech). The peer set includes both near-peer ($300–$500M) and outlier valuations (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B).

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $208M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $300M. This range reflects meaningful methodological divergence: stage-progression implies conservative advancement, while peer-median reflects observed market pricing for comparable-stage climate companies. Key uncertainties include INERATEC's technology differentiation relative to peers, capital efficiency trajectory, and the degree to which outlier valuations in the comp set reflect genuine comparable conditions versus sector exuberance.
